frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: value of self-protection updated by discrepancy between current and previously estimated feedback

frameworks.name: developmental cognitive neuroscience
frameworks.one_line: age-related shift in how social feedback is integrated into self-protective decisions

core_question: How does the medial prefrontal cortex develop to support self-protective shifts driven by accumulated versus current negative social feedback?
key_assumptions: self-protective bias is quantifiable in a reciprocal evaluation task
VMPFC and RMPFC support dissociable feedback-integration timescales
value of self-protection can be tracked with a reinforcement-learning update
manipulations: accumulated history of negative social evaluation
key_findings: youths bias self-protectively to current negative feedback
adults bias self-protectively to accumulated negative feedback
VMPFC tracks both current and accumulated feedback effects
RMPFC exclusively tracks longer feedback history
interpretations: RMPFC-VMPFC with PPC drive developmental increase in deep-history self-protection bias
feedback integration depth reflects PFC maturation computing prediction-error-like discrepancies
